files.diff:
diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index 69a806b..edba692 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -7,39 +7,39 @@
 - Query results truncate at 5k. Always page by `customer_id > last LIMIT 5000` for by-customer promos, active rows, issues, and enterprise leads.
 - Direct `SELECT s.seat_count` may be omitted from row dict unless aliased (`AS seats`); aggregates still work.
-- Active individual subs use `subscriptions.status='subscribed'`. Dashboard open issues = active subscribed customers' issues; raw `issues` includes stale old tickets.
-- In-app ads hurt quality; keep `ads_strength=0`. Social includes quota/rate-limit/support complaints: do **not** cut quotas.
+- Active individual subs use `subscriptions.status='subscribed'`. Dashboard open issues = active subscribed customers' issues; raw `issues` includes large stale tickets for cancelled subs.
+- In-app ads hurt quality; keep `ads_strength=0`. Social includes quota/rate-limit/support complaints: do **not** cut quotas. Avoid B tier downgrade: earlier B3 caused churn cliff; B4 retention is much better despite compute cost.
 
-## State at Week56 start (Day392) before actions
-- Cash **$4.063M**. Active individuals **104,560**; enterprise seats **0**. Week56 metrics: usage **158.6M/wk**, new small subs 92, cancellations **1,900**, no outage/overload, peak daily usage **27.6M** vs tier7 300M (tier6 would be 80M), P95 peak 215ms, error peak 0.52%.
-- Active book: S2 B 59,440 ($9.806M/mo current eff), S3 B 21,426 ($2.965M), D_S07 B 22,443 ($1.904M), D_S10 B 542 ($35k), S3 C 688 ($298k). D_S07 is close to compute break-even (36.5M tier4 units/wk ≈$438k/wk compute), so avoid extra retention credits there unless needed for reputation.
-- Active issues **2,221 all age0**: D_S07 1,340; S2 502; S3 303; D_S10 76. Raw old tickets mostly belong inactive subs. Social Day392 still negative about S3 support/rate-limit blockers; competitor event +0.186 quality pressure appeared.
-- Last7 churn: D_S10 B age240 605 (mostly run-off), S2 B age300 316, S3 B age270 248, S2 B age270 120, S2 B age240 98, S3 B age240 89, D_S07 B age270 74/age240 71. B4 retention much better than B3 cliff but support/social still matters.
-- Enterprise: after Day385 mass refresh, still **0 subscribed/accepted ever**. Latest open lead state before actions: 34,315 old agent-latest Day385 leads (22.77M seats) plus 3,512 new system-latest leads from Day386-392 (2.20M seats). Avg response estimates are long (E2 12d, E3 22d, D_E07 19d), so do **not** refresh old agent-latest leads every week; let them mature.
-- R&D in progress: t3 completes ~Day425 (+0.0711), t6 ~Day449 (+0.3437); t10/t11/t12 after sim end. No new R&D unless cash/enterprise conversion improves.
+## State at Week57 start (Day399) before actions
+- Cash **$5.187M**. Active individuals **103,722**; enterprise subscribed seats **0**. Week57 metrics: usage **166.6M/wk**, new small subs 146, cancellations **984** (improved from 1,900), no outage/overload, daily usage max **29.1M** vs tier6 cap 80M/tier7 cap 300M, P95 226ms, error 0.54%.
+- Active book: S2 B 58,983; S3 B 21,363; D_S07 B 22,161; D_S10 B 468; S3 C 726; S2 C 21. Current configured monthly gross before future churn after Week57 promos: S2 B ~$10.40M, S3 B ~$3.15M, D_S07 B ~$2.15M, S3 C ~$0.33M. D_S07/S3 B are thin after tier4 compute; avoid extra credits there except issue goodwill/reputation.
+- Active issues **2,608 all age0**: D_S07 1,599; S2 520; S3 415; D_S10 74. Ops resolved prior issues same day, but social Day399 still has severe S2/S3 support-SLA complaints citing 72h/5d/old tickets; raw stale issues are mostly cancelled subs and still create reputational noise.
+- Competitor/social pressure high this week: posts reported several quality boosts (~0.1869, 0.1663, 0.1239, 0.1298, 0.0808, 0.1002). Delivered quality remains high (B S2 20.45, S3 21.86, D_S07 19.94) but watch churn after events.
+- Enterprise still **0 subscribed/accepted ever**. Before actions: 364,454 old agent-latest leads (191.0M seats, mostly Day385 or earlier, no recent accepted/lost) plus 2,577 new system-latest leads from Days393-399 (1.815M seats). Old Day392 offers (3,512 leads/2.20M seats) also not accepted yet. Do not refresh old agent-latest leads; only answer new system/customer turns.
+- R&D in progress: t3 completes ~Day425 (+0.0711), t6 ~Day449 (+0.3437); t10/t11/t12 after sim end. No new R&D unless cash/enterprise improves.
 
-## Week56 actions applied (Day392)
-- Kept capacity **tier7** for one more week as insurance while Day385 enterprise offers mature; if Day399 still zero enterprise and peak <70M/day, reconsider tier6.
-- Kept prices/model/quotas **A149/B239/C549, A2/B4/C5, quotas 3k/15k/60k**, no ads, ads_strength 0, lead promos 0, dev 5k/day global.
-- Rebalanced support: global ops 20k/day + targeted ops **S2 25k, S3 25k, D_S07 30k, D_S10 2k** = **102k/day total ops**. Rationale: S3 support complaints need more coverage; D_S07 issue volume still high.
+## Week57 actions applied (Day399)
+- Downscaled capacity **tier7 -> tier6** (80M/day) because no enterprise seats and individual peak <30M/day; saves **$329k/wk**. If enterprise seats arrive or peak >70M/day, go back to tier7 immediately next week.
+- Kept prices/model/quotas **A149/B239/C549, A2/B4/C5, quotas 3k/15k/60k**, no ads, `ads_strength=0`, lead promos 0, dev **$5k/day** global, no targeted dev.
+- Increased support while net-saving via capacity: global ops **$20k/day** + targeted ops **S2 $30k, S3 $40k, D_S07 $30k, D_S10 $2k** = **$122k/day total ops**. Reason: public S2/S3 SLA complaints are the main retention/reputation risk; D_S07 issue volume remains high.
 - Rebuilt ongoing promos preserving group bridges: S2 $60, S3 $90, D_S07 $100 + B $40, D_S10 $90 + B $80, dormant discovered bridges unchanged.
-- Rebuilt by-customer promos to **26,173 customers**, potential credit sum **$300,130**: active open issue +$25 (2,221), next7 old-B renewal +$10 for **S2/S3 only** (23,933; D_S07/D_S10 excluded for low margin), next7 first-B +$50 for S2/S3 (61), mature S2/S3 C +$25 (89). Value dist: 23,817@$10; 2,179@$25; 116@$35; 46@$50; 15@$75.
-- Enterprise: replied only to the **3,512 new system leads** (2.20M seats) with lower test offers; all succeeded. Offers: E2 A59/B89/C179; E3 A49/B89/C159; D_E07 A59/B119/C209; D_E03/D_E08 A59/B129/C219; D_E02 A39/B79/C149; D_E10 A29/B59/C109; D_E01 A59/B119/C219; D_E04 A69/B139/C229. Did **not** refresh old Day385 agent-latest leads.
-- Social: replied to S3 support post #1568 with agent post #57: 24/7 triage, 12h auto-escalation, in-product status/rate-limit guidance, renewal credits for blocked users.
+- Rebuilt by-customer promos to **8,187 customers**, potential credit sum **$124,565**: active open issue +$25 (2,608), next7 old-B renewal +$10 for **S2/S3 only** (5,369; D_S07/D_S10 excluded for low margin), next7 first-B +$50 for S2/S3 (11), mature S2/S3 C +$25 (205). Value dist: 5,369@$10; 2,807@$25; 5@$50; 6@$75.
+- Enterprise: replied to all **2,577 new system leads** (1.815M seats), all succeeded. Lower test offers: E2 A49/B79/C179; E3 A39/B79/C149; D_E07 A49/B109/C199; D_E03/D_E08 A49/B119/C209; D_E02 A29/B69/C139; D_E10 A25/B55/C119; D_E01 A49/B109/C199; D_E04 A59/B129/C219; other low-WTP groups A25-29/B55-69/C109-139. Still do **not** refresh old agent-latest leads unless customer/system sends a new turn.
+- Social: replied to support post #1610 with agent post #58: uptime without fast support is not reliability; expanded S2/S3 support pods, written 12h critical-ticket SLA, daily status, auto-credit blockers >24h, weekly response metrics.
 
-## Forecast/cash model after Week56 actions
-- Configured individual gross bill schedule under current promos before churn/future cancels: next7 **$4.534M**, next14 **$5.564M**, next28 **$13.997M**, next30 **$16.006M**, next84 **$42.135M**. Next7 top receipts: S2 B $3.089M; S3 B $0.780M; D_S07 B $0.614M.
-- Weekly no-enterprise cost at current settings: compute ~$1.9-2.0M, ops **$0.714M**, capacity **$0.525M**, dev $0.035M, lead costs ~$0.003M = **~$3.2M/wk**. No-enterprise +1wk cash point ≈ **$5.3-5.5M** after scheduled billing/churn.
-- Enterprise remains dominant uncertainty. Base forecasts assume no/limited enterprise because accepted seats have been zero historically, but upper CI must allow delayed acceptance from 22.8M old seats + 2.2M new seats.
+## Forecast/cash model after Week57 actions
+- Configured individual gross bill schedule under current promos before churn/future cancels: next7 **$0.971M**, next14 **$3.004M**, next28 **$15.086M**, next30 **$16.075M**, next84 **$44.502M**. Next7 is a low-billing week; top receipts: S2 B $0.814M, S3 C $0.088M, S3 B $0.067M.
+- Weekly no-enterprise cost after actions: compute ~$2.04M, ops **$0.854M**, capacity tier6 **$0.196M**, dev $0.035M, lead cost ~$0.004M = **~$3.13M/wk**. No-enterprise +1wk cash point ≈ **$3.0M** after low scheduled billing/churn.
+- Enterprise remains dominant uncertainty, but conversion has been zero historically. Base forecasts assume no/limited enterprise; CI upper bounds still allow delayed acceptance from old 191M-seat pipeline + recent 4.0M seats.
 
-## Strategy / next checks for Day399
-- First check cash vs ~$5.4M no-enterprise forecast, enterprise subscribed seats/usage, latest enterprise turns (customer/system pending), peak usage, issue ages, social sentiment, and churn by S2/S3/D_S07 old-B cohorts.
-- If enterprise seats >0 or peak >70M/day, keep tier7 and compute gross margin by plan/group. If still zero enterprise and peak <70M/day, consider downscale to tier6 to save $329k/wk.
-- If support posts improve and active issues remain same-day, trim ops cautiously toward 90-95k/day; if S3/D_S07 issue ages/social worsen, hold or increase S3 ops.
+## Strategy / next checks for Day406
+- First check cash vs ~$3.0M no-enterprise forecast, enterprise subscribed seats/usage, latest enterprise turns (customer/system pending), peak daily usage/overload, active issue ages, social reaction to post #58, and churn by S2/S3/D_S07 old-B cohorts.
+- If enterprise seats >0 or peak >70M/day, restore tier7 and compute gross margin by plan/group. If still zero enterprise and peak <70M/day, stay tier6.
+- If active issues remain same-day and support sentiment improves, consider trimming ops slowly toward 105-115k/day; if S2/S3 support posts or issue ages worsen, hold/increase S2/S3 ops.
 - Rebuild by-customer promos weekly with paged queries. Keep issue credits while support complaints persist. Keep old-B credits focused on profitable S2/S3; avoid extra D_S07/D_S10 renewal credits unless reputation risk spikes.
-- Enterprise: answer only new system/customer leads; do not reset old agent-latest leads unless there is evidence lower prices or customer turns require it. If still no acceptances after Day399/406, treat enterprise conversion probability as near-zero and operate for individual cash margin.
+- Enterprise: answer only new system/customer leads. If still no acceptances by Day406/413, treat enterprise conversion probability as near-zero and optimize for individual cash margin.
 
-## Forecasts intended for Week56 next-week submission
-- +1wk cash point **$5.5M**, CI **$2.5M to $55.0M**.
-- +4wk point **$7.0M**, CI **$0.5M to $150.0M**.
-- +12wk point **$12.0M**, CI **-$3.0M to $300.0M**.
-- +26wk point **$20.0M**, CI **-$8.0M to $600.0M**.
+## Forecasts intended for Week57 next-week submission
+- +1wk cash point **$3.05M**, CI **$2.10M to $30.0M**.
+- +4wk point **$7.2M**, CI **$1.5M to $100.0M**.
+- +12wk point **$10.0M**, CI **-$2.0M to $250.0M**.
+- +26wk point **$12.0M**, CI **-$5.0M to $400.0M**.

files.content:
import novamind_api as nm, json
from collections import defaultdict, Counter
DAY = nm.vars.current_day
print('Week57 actions at day', DAY)

def q(sql):
    r = nm.query(sql)
    if r.get('warning'):
        print('WARNING', r['warning'])
    return r['rows']

# Stable pricing/model/quotas: B tier 4 remains necessary for retention; no quota cuts.
for name, func, kwargs in [
    ('prices', nm.pricing.set_prices, dict(A=149.0, B=239.0, C=549.0)),
    ('model_tiers', nm.pricing.set_model_tiers, dict(A=2, B=4, C=5)),
    ('quotas', nm.pricing.set_usage_quotas, dict(A=3000, B=15000, C=60000)),
]:
    try:
        print(name, func(**kwargs))
    except Exception as e:
        print(name, 'error', e)

# Capacity: no enterprise seats for another week and individual peak <30M/day; tier 6 has 80M/day headroom and saves $329k/week.
try:
    print('capacity', nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=6))
except Exception as e:
    print('capacity error', e)

# Spend: keep no ads/in-app ads. Increase human support on S2/S3 while preserving D_S07 same-day throughput.
try:
    print('daily_spend', nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=20000, development=5000))
except Exception as e:
    print('daily_spend error', e)
try:
    # global ops 20k + targets = 122k/day total. Capacity savings more than offset this week's support increase.
    print('targeted_ops', nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={"S2":30000.0,"S3":40000.0,"D_S07":30000.0,"D_S10":2000.0}))
except Exception as e:
    print('targeted_ops error', e)
try:
    print('targeted_dev', nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend={}))
except Exception as e:
    print('targeted_dev error', e)
try:
    print('targeted_ads', nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={"content_marketing":{},"social_media":{},"search_ads":{},"linkedin":{},"referral_program":{}}))
except Exception as e:
    print('targeted_ads error', e)
try:
    print('ads_strength', nm.marketing.set_ads_strength(global_strength=0.0, by_group={}, by_customer={}))
except Exception as e:
    print('ads_strength error', e)
try:
    print('lead_promo', nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group={"S1":0.0,"S2":0.0,"S3":0.0,"D_S01":0.0,"D_S02":0.0,"D_S03":0.0,"D_S04":0.0,"D_S05":0.0,"D_S06":0.0,"D_S07":0.0,"D_S08":0.0,"D_S09":0.0,"D_S10":0.0}, by_channel={}, by_channel_group={}))
except Exception as e:
    print('lead_promo error', e)

# Rebuild retention/customer credits for the next billing week. Preserve standing group bridge promos.
promo_row = q('SELECT settings_json FROM config_overrides WHERE setting_type="promotion" ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1')
if promo_row:
    cur = json.loads(promo_row[0]['settings_json'])
    by_group = cur.get('by_group', {})
    by_group_plan = cur.get('by_group_plan', {})
else:
    by_group = {"S2":60.0,"S3":90.0,"D_S04":100.0,"D_S07":100.0,"D_S10":90.0,"S1":125.0,"D_S03":125.0,"D_S05":50.0,"D_S06":120.0,"D_S01":100.0,"D_S02":100.0,"D_S08":110.0,"D_S09":100.0}
    by_group_plan = {"S2":{"A":40.0},"S3":{"A":20.0},"D_S04":{"B":80.0},"D_S07":{"A":20.0,"B":40.0},"D_S10":{"B":80.0},"D_S06":{"B":90.0},"D_S01":{"B":80.0},"D_S02":{"B":80.0},"D_S08":{"B":80.0},"D_S09":{"B":80.0}}
mods = [(DAY+i)%30 for i in range(1,8)]
mods_str = ','.join(str(m) for m in mods)
by_customer = defaultdict(float)
counts = Counter()
# Active open issues: goodwill credit for current subscribers with blockers.
last = -1
while True:
    rows = q(f"""
    SELECT DISTINCT s.customer_id
    FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
    JOIN issues i ON i.customer_id=s.customer_id AND i.status='open'
    WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.customer_id>{last}
    ORDER BY s.customer_id LIMIT 5000
    """)
    if not rows: break
    for r in rows:
        by_customer[str(r['customer_id'])] += 25.0
        counts['active_issue_25'] += 1
    last = rows[-1]['customer_id']
    if len(rows) < 5000: break
# Next-7 old B renewals: profitable S2/S3 only. D_S07/D_S10 extra credits remain excluded for margin.
last = -1
while True:
    rows = q(f"""
    SELECT s.customer_id
    FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
    WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.plan='B'
      AND c.group_id IN ('S2','S3')
      AND s.billing_day_mod30 IN ({mods_str})
      AND ({DAY} - s.start_day) >= 180
      AND s.customer_id>{last}
    ORDER BY s.customer_id LIMIT 5000
    """)
    if not rows: break
    for r in rows:
        by_customer[str(r['customer_id'])] += 10.0
        counts['next7_old_B_S2S3_10'] += 1
    last = rows[-1]['customer_id']
    if len(rows) < 5000: break
# First B renewal bridge for new S2/S3 B subscribers.
last = -1
while True:
    rows = q(f"""
    SELECT s.customer_id, s.start_day, ((s.billing_day_mod30 - ({DAY}%30) + 30) % 30) AS days_until
    FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
    WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.plan='B'
      AND c.group_id IN ('S2','S3')
      AND s.billing_day_mod30 IN ({mods_str})
      AND s.customer_id>{last}
    ORDER BY s.customer_id LIMIT 5000
    """)
    if not rows: break
    for r in rows:
        bill_age = DAY + int(r['days_until']) - int(r['start_day'])
        if 25 <= bill_age <= 45:
            by_customer[str(r['customer_id'])] += 50.0
            counts['next7_first_B_S2S3_50'] += 1
    last = rows[-1]['customer_id']
    if len(rows) < 5000: break
# Mature C users in S2/S3: high-value renewal bridge.
last = -1
while True:
    rows = q(f"""
    SELECT s.customer_id, s.start_day, ((s.billing_day_mod30 - ({DAY}%30) + 30) % 30) AS days_until
    FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
    WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.plan='C'
      AND c.group_id IN ('S2','S3') AND s.billing_day_mod30 IN ({mods_str})
      AND s.customer_id>{last}
    ORDER BY s.customer_id LIMIT 5000
    """)
    if not rows: break
    for r in rows:
        bill_age = DAY + int(r['days_until']) - int(r['start_day'])
        if bill_age >= 210:
            by_customer[str(r['customer_id'])] += 25.0
            counts['next7_mature_C_25'] += 1
    last = rows[-1]['customer_id']
    if len(rows) < 5000: break
by_customer = {k: float(v) for k,v in by_customer.items() if v > 0}
print('promo component counts', dict(counts))
print('promo by_customer_count', len(by_customer), 'sum_if_all_bill', sum(by_customer.values()), 'value_dist', dict(Counter(by_customer.values())))
try:
    res = nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group=by_group, by_customer=by_customer, by_group_plan=by_group_plan)
    print('promotion set: by_customer_count', len(by_customer))
except Exception as e:
    print('promotion error', e)
    raise

# Enterprise: answer only latest system leads from this week. Lower test offers modestly but keep compute-positive economics.
price_by_group = {
    'E2': [('A',49.0),('B',79.0),('C',179.0)],
    'E3': [('A',39.0),('B',79.0),('C',149.0)],
    'D_E07': [('A',49.0),('B',109.0),('C',199.0)],
    'D_E03': [('A',49.0),('B',119.0),('C',209.0)],
    'D_E08': [('A',49.0),('B',119.0),('C',209.0)],
    'D_E02': [('A',29.0),('B',69.0),('C',139.0)],
    'D_E10': [('A',25.0),('B',55.0),('C',119.0)],
    'D_E01': [('A',49.0),('B',109.0),('C',199.0)],
    'D_E04': [('A',59.0),('B',129.0),('C',219.0)],
    'D_E05': [('A',29.0),('B',69.0),('C',139.0)],
    'D_E06': [('A',25.0),('B',55.0),('C',109.0)],
    'D_E09': [('A',25.0),('B',55.0),('C',109.0)],
    'E1': [('A',25.0),('B',55.0),('C',109.0)],
}
def offers_for(g):
    return [[p, price] for p, price in price_by_group.get(g, [('A',39.0),('B',79.0),('C',149.0)])]
new_rows=[]; last=-1
while True:
    rows=q(f"""
    WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)
    SELECT s.customer_id, c.group_id, s.seat_count AS seats, c.created_day, et.day AS turn_day
    FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
    JOIN latest l USING(customer_id) JOIN enterprise_turns et ON et.message_id=l.mid
    WHERE c.customer_type='large' AND s.status='lead' AND et.sender='system' AND s.customer_id>{last}
    ORDER BY s.customer_id LIMIT 5000
    """)
    if not rows: break
    new_rows.extend(rows)
    last=rows[-1]['customer_id']
    if len(rows)<5000: break
print('enterprise system leads fetched', len(new_rows), 'seats', sum((r.get('seats') or 0) for r in new_rows), 'groups', dict(Counter(r['group_id'] for r in new_rows)), 'turn_days', dict(Counter(r['turn_day'] for r in new_rows)))
sent=ok=fail=0; fail_samples=[]
chunk_size=1000
for i in range(0, len(new_rows), chunk_size):
    chunk=new_rows[i:i+chunk_size]
    deals=[[int(r['customer_id']), offers_for(r['group_id'])] for r in chunk]
    try:
        res=nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=deals)
        results=res.get('results', []) if isinstance(res, dict) else []
        ok_chunk=sum(1 for x in results if x.get('success'))
        bad=[x for x in results if not x.get('success')]
        sent+=len(deals); ok+=ok_chunk; fail+=len(bad); fail_samples.extend(bad[:5])
        print(f'enterprise chunk {i//chunk_size+1}: deals={len(deals)} ok={ok_chunk} fail={len(bad)}')
    except Exception as e:
        sent+=len(deals); fail+=len(deals); fail_samples.append({'chunk':i//chunk_size+1,'error':str(e)})
        print('enterprise chunk error', i//chunk_size+1, e)
print('enterprise total sent', sent, 'ok', ok, 'fail', fail, 'fail_samples', fail_samples[:10])

# Social: reply to newest support critique with a concrete SLA/metrics commitment.
msg = "You’re right: uptime without fast support isn’t reliability. We expanded S2/S3 support pods, made critical tickets a written 12h-response SLA with daily status updates, and auto-credit blockers >24h. We’ll publish response metrics weekly."
try:
    res = nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=msg, reply_to_post_id=1610)
    print('social', res)
except Exception as e:
    print('social error', e)

# Validation snippets
print('\nvalidation config latest')
for r in q('SELECT tool_name, setting_type, day, substr(settings_json,1,300) settings_json FROM config_overrides WHERE id IN (SELECT max(id) FROM config_overrides GROUP BY setting_type) ORDER BY setting_type'):
    print(r)
print('\nvalidation enterprise latest sender counts')
for r in q("""
WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT et.sender, et.day, COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.seat_count) seats
FROM latest l JOIN enterprise_turns et ON et.message_id=l.mid JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id)
WHERE et.day>=392
GROUP BY et.sender, et.day ORDER BY et.day, et.sender
"""):
    print(r)
print('Week57 actions complete')

import novamind_api as nm, json, math
from collections import defaultdict, Counter
DAY=nm.vars.current_day
print('DAY', DAY)

def q(sql):
    r=nm.query(sql)
    if r.get('warning'):
        print('WARNING', r['warning'])
    return r['rows']

# Promo settings and billing forecast
print('\n## billing schedule with current configured promos')
try:
    promo=json.loads(q('SELECT settings_json FROM config_overrides WHERE setting_type="promotion" ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1')[0]['settings_json'])
except Exception:
    promo={'global':0,'by_group':{},'by_group_plan':{},'by_customer':{}}
print('promo summary', {k:(len(v) if isinstance(v,dict) else v) for k,v in promo.items()})

def current_promo(cid,g,p):
    cid=str(cid)
    return (promo.get('global',0) or 0) + promo.get('by_group',{}).get(g,0) + promo.get('by_group_plan',{}).get(g,{}).get(p,0) + promo.get('by_customer',{}).get(cid,0)
for horizon in [7,14,28,30,56,84]:
    last=-1; total=0.0; bill_events=0; bygp=defaultdict(float); bycnt=Counter(); promtot=0.0
    while True:
        rr=q(f"""
        SELECT s.customer_id,c.group_id,s.plan,s.listed_price,s.effective_price,s.seat_count AS seats,s.billing_day_mod30,s.start_day
        FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
        WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.customer_id>{last}
        ORDER BY s.customer_id LIMIT 5000
        """)
        if not rr: break
        for r in rr:
            bills=sum(1 for i in range(1,horizon+1) if (DAY+i)%30==r['billing_day_mod30'])
            if bills:
                pr=current_promo(r['customer_id'],r['group_id'],r['plan'])
                eff=max(float(r['listed_price'])-pr,0)*int(r.get('seats') or 1)*bills
                total += eff
                promtot += pr*int(r.get('seats') or 1)*bills
                bill_events += bills
                bygp[(r['group_id'],r['plan'])]+=eff
                bycnt[(r['group_id'],r['plan'])]+=bills
        last=rr[-1]['customer_id']
        if len(rr)<5000: break
    print('horizon',horizon,'configured_gross',round(total,2),'bill_events',bill_events,'promo_sum_if_bill',round(promtot,2),'top',[(k,bycnt[k],round(v,2)) for k,v in sorted(bygp.items(), key=lambda x:-x[1])[:10]])

print('\n## next7 bill by day/group')
for i in range(1,8):
    mod=(DAY+i)%30
    day=DAY+i
    last=-1; bygp=defaultdict(float); cnt=Counter(); total=0
    while True:
        rr=q(f"""
        SELECT s.customer_id,c.group_id,s.plan,s.listed_price,s.seat_count AS seats,s.billing_day_mod30
        FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
        WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.billing_day_mod30={mod} AND s.customer_id>{last}
        ORDER BY s.customer_id LIMIT 5000
        """)
        if not rr: break
        for r in rr:
            eff=max(float(r['listed_price'])-current_promo(r['customer_id'],r['group_id'],r['plan']),0)*int(r.get('seats') or 1)
            total+=eff; bygp[(r['group_id'],r['plan'])]+=eff; cnt[(r['group_id'],r['plan'])]+=1
        last=rr[-1]['customer_id']
        if len(rr)<5000: break
    print(day,'mod',mod,'total',round(total,2),'top',[(k,cnt[k],round(v,2)) for k,v in sorted(bygp.items(), key=lambda x:-x[1])[:8]])

print('\n## cancellations last7 detail')

FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)

import novamind_api as nm,json,math
DAY=nm.vars.current_day

def q(sql):
 r=nm.query(sql)
 if r.get('warning'): print('WARN',r['warning'])
 return r['rows']
# cash from ledger total
rows=q('SELECT SUM(amount) cash FROM ledger')
print('cash ledger',rows)
# promo settings
promo=json.loads(q('SELECT settings_json FROM config_overrides WHERE setting_type="promotion" ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1')[0]['settings_json'])
mods=[(DAY+i)%30 for i in range(1,8)]
mods_str=','.join(map(str,mods))
# compute planned revenue by day next 30 active small with current configured promos
plans=[]
for horizon in [7,14,28,30,84]:
    # mods for days 1..horizon; if >30 one full cycles plus extras, approximate by billed cycles count per customer? For each customer, bill days in horizon = number of i 1..h with (DAY+i)%30 == billing_mod
    rows=[]; last=-1; total=0; bygp={}; n=0
    while True:
        # selecting all active small in pages; compute bill_count in python
        rr=q(f"""
        SELECT s.customer_id,c.group_id,s.plan,s.listed_price,s.seat_count AS seats,s.billing_day_mod30,s.start_day
        FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
        WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.customer_id>{last}
        ORDER BY s.customer_id LIMIT 5000
        """)
        if not rr: break
        for r in rr:
            bill_count=sum(1 for i in range(1,horizon+1) if (DAY+i)%30==r['billing_day_mod30'])
            if bill_count:
                cid=str(r['customer_id']); g=r['group_id']; p=r['plan']
                prom=promo.get('global',0)+promo.get('by_group',{}).get(g,0)+promo.get('by_group_plan',{}).get(g,{}).get(p,0)+promo.get('by_customer',{}).get(cid,0)
                eff=max(float(r['listed_price'])-prom,0)*int(r.get('seats',1))*bill_count
                total+=eff; n+=bill_count
                bygp[(g,p)]=bygp.get((g,p),0)+eff
        last=rr[-1]['customer_id']
        if len(rr)<5000: break
    print('configured small bill gross horizon',horizon,'total',total,'bill_events',n,'top',sorted([(v,k) for k,v in bygp.items()], reverse=True)[:10])
# next7 details by group
rows=[]; last=-1; details={}; count={}
while True:
    rr=q(f"""
    SELECT s.customer_id,c.group_id,s.plan,s.listed_price,s.seat_count AS seats,s.billing_day_mod30,s.start_day
    FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
    WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.billing_day_mod30 IN ({mods_str}) AND s.customer_id>{last}
    ORDER BY s.customer_id LIMIT 5000
    """)
    if not rr: break
    for r in rr:
        cid=str(r['customer_id']); g=r['group_id']; p=r['plan']
        prom=promo.get('global',0)+promo.get('by_group',{}).get(g,0)+promo.get('by_group_plan',{}).get(g,{}).get(p,0)+promo.get('by_customer',{}).get(cid,0)
        eff=max(float(r['listed_price'])-prom,0)*int(r.get('seats',1))
        details[(g,p)]=details.get((g,p),0)+eff
        count[(g,p)]=count.get((g,p),0)+1
    last=rr[-1]['customer_id']
    if len(rr)<5000: break
print('next7 configured by gp')
for k,v in sorted(details.items(), key=lambda x:-x[1]): print(k,count[k],v)
# last 7/14 costs
for days in [7,14,30]:
    rows=q(f"SELECT category, SUM(amount) amt FROM ledger WHERE day>{DAY-days} AND day<={DAY} GROUP BY category")
    print('ledger',days,rows, 'total', sum(r['amt'] for r in rows))
# usage costs by active planned tiers last7
rows=q(f"""
SELECT c.group_id,s.plan,SUM(u.usage_units) usage
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) JOIN daily_usage u USING(customer_id)
WHERE u.day>{DAY-7} AND u.day<={DAY} AND s.status='subscribed'
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan ORDER BY usage DESC
""")
print('usage active last7',rows)
# cancellation rates last7 by bill cohort approximate: cancels over active+cancels for groups
rows=q(f"""
SELECT c.group_id,s.plan,CAST((s.end_day-s.start_day)/30 AS INT)*30 age,COUNT(*) n
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status='cancelled' AND s.end_day>{DAY-7} AND s.end_day<={DAY} AND c.customer_type='small'
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan,age ORDER BY n DESC LIMIT 50
""")
print('cancel last7',rows)
